visualizerParams.title: Using `str.capitalize()` Built-in
visualizerParams.problemDescription: The provided code manually capitalizes the first character using slicing and string concatenation. Refactor it to use Python's built-in `str.capitalize()` method, which is the standard and most concise way to perform this operation.

solution.explanation: Python's `capitalize()` method automatically handles empty strings and ensures the first character is uppercase while all subsequent characters are lowercase. Note: if the original intent was to ONLY change the first character, manual slicing might still be preferred, but `capitalize()` is the most common built-in for this task.
